frameworks.name: functional states of working memory
frameworks.one_line: distinct active vs latent neural states encode prioritized vs concurrently held WM items

frameworks.name: evidence integration / decision dynamics
frameworks.one_line: cognitive modeling of moment-to-moment evidence accumulation linked to WM state

frameworks.name: neural pattern analysis of EEG
frameworks.one_line: multivariate decoding of WM contents from human electrophysiology

frameworks.name: priority-based attentional gating in WM
frameworks.one_line: selection mechanism that shifts items between active and latent status

core_question: How do different functional states of working memory contribute to using stored items to guide ongoing decisions?
key_assumptions: WM items can occupy distinct functional states depending on task priority
prioritized vs concurrently held items engage dissociable neural codes
spontaneous and stimulus-evoked activity index different WM states
manipulations: priority shifts between two concurrently maintained WM items
prolonged maintenance period with intervening decisions
comparison of selected vs unselected memory during delay
key_findings: selected memories appear in spontaneous delay-period activity (active state)
concurrently held memories appear only in stimulus-evoked activity (latent state)
active state tracks moment-to-moment evidence integration quality and interference
priority can shift back and forth without degrading memory precision
interpretations: WM items occupy a hierarchy of functional states that flexibly map to behavioral priority
only the active state engages ongoing decision dynamics; the latent state preserves precision for later use
